results.identified_issue: Read the request as being about the question CARD specifically ('we don't need to lock the question'), not the arguments. Implemented it as a floor on the existing per-question lock (lockFloorIndex = first argument, index 1) applied inside maxAllowedIndex, rather than seeding unlockedIndex=1 in select/restore/initial — one choke point, so persistence, restore, and re-entering a question all inherit it without three edits that could drift. Deliberately left the argument-to-argument gate intact: the reporter only asked to unlock the question, and 'you should still read' means keep the prompt readable, not remove all gating. If they actually wanted the WHOLE question (every argument) to scroll freely, this is the wrong scope and the floor should instead be removed entirely / unlockedIndex opened to pages.count-1. Not built (flow forbids builds); reasoned through the unlock/advance/bounce paths rather than run it.
results.summary: Made the question card scroll-through instead of a listen-first lock
results.detailed_description: You were right that the question shouldn't be a toll gate. Before this, entering a question dropped you on its card and locked the feed there — you had to let the whole prompt read out before you could scroll to the first argument. I removed that specific lock: the question card no longer gates the feed, so you can swipe straight through onto the first viewpoint the moment a question opens. The question still reads aloud on its own when you land on it, and a tap replays it, so listening stays an option — it's just no longer required. The per-argument lock (hear one viewpoint before the next unlocks) is untouched, since your note was only about the question itself. Mechanically: I gave the feed's lock a floor at the first argument (lockFloorIndex) so the highest landable page is never below the first viewpoint, and updated the QuestionIntroCard/DiscoverView docs that said 'you can't scroll past it until it's read.' I couldn't run it in this pass (the queue flow builds on the device, not here), so I traced the unlock, hands-free-advance, and bounce-back paths by hand rather than on a simulator — worth a quick check on device that swiping past the question lands cleanly on the first argument and the argument gate still bounces you back past that.

description: The question, we don't need to lock the question. You should just be able to scroll right through a question. But you should still read, so this should be an option to listen to.
